raw: {"score": 3, "rationale": "Clean enough — solidly hits the level 3 anchors but does not reach level 4. Strengths: clear backend layering (db/, http/, policy/, services/), no circular imports and no orphans (madge.json shows empty arrays), all business constants centralized in policy/index.ts (MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS=5, LOAN_PERIOD_DAYS=14, LATE_FINE_RATE_CENTS_PER_DAY=50, LATE_FINE_CAP_CENTS=1000, RESERVATION_NOTIFICATION_HOURS=48, FINE_BORROW_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_CENTS=500) and consumed via imports — loanService.ts uses canBorrow, computeFineCents, dueDateFromBorrow rather than redefining values. Domain errors form a named hierarchy in errors.ts (ConflictError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, PaymentRequiredError) used consistently. Frontend has shared API types defined once in api/types.ts. No production source file exceeds 300 lines (largest non-test: routes.ts at 212, loanService.ts at 202; pages top out around 272). Why not 4: (a) services call raw db.prepare with inline SQL strings throughout — e.g., loanService.ts:39-41, 64-69, 71-74, 93-95, 98-100, 134-136, 153-156 — there is no thin typed DB-access abstraction, so services aren't unit-testable in isolation from SQLite. (b) The frontend is organized strictly by technical role (api/, components/, pages/, utils/) rather than by feature — the rubric calls this out explicitly as the level-3-vs-4 discriminator; CheckoutPage.tsx (252 lines) lives next to BooksPage and MembersPage with no checkout/ feature folder owning its components, hooks, and API calls. (c) No shared types boundary between backend and frontend — frontend api/types.ts is a separate redeclaration of backend types.ts rather than a shared package or generated client. The two large test files (604 and 486 lines) are excluded by the rubric's note that test quality is out of scope."}

parsed.rationale: Clean enough — solidly hits the level 3 anchors but does not reach level 4. Strengths: clear backend layering (db/, http/, policy/, services/), no circular imports and no orphans (madge.json shows empty arrays), all business constants centralized in policy/index.ts (MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS=5, LOAN_PERIOD_DAYS=14, LATE_FINE_RATE_CENTS_PER_DAY=50, LATE_FINE_CAP_CENTS=1000, RESERVATION_NOTIFICATION_HOURS=48, FINE_BORROW_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_CENTS=500) and consumed via imports — loanService.ts uses canBorrow, computeFineCents, dueDateFromBorrow rather than redefining values. Domain errors form a named hierarchy in errors.ts (ConflictError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, PaymentRequiredError) used consistently. Frontend has shared API types defined once in api/types.ts. No production source file exceeds 300 lines (largest non-test: routes.ts at 212, loanService.ts at 202; pages top out around 272). Why not 4: (a) services call raw db.prepare with inline SQL strings throughout — e.g., loanService.ts:39-41, 64-69, 71-74, 93-95, 98-100, 134-136, 153-156 — there is no thin typed DB-access abstraction, so services aren't unit-testable in isolation from SQLite. (b) The frontend is organized strictly by technical role (api/, components/, pages/, utils/) rather than by feature — the rubric calls this out explicitly as the level-3-vs-4 discriminator; CheckoutPage.tsx (252 lines) lives next to BooksPage and MembersPage with no checkout/ feature folder owning its components, hooks, and API calls. (c) No shared types boundary between backend and frontend — frontend api/types.ts is a separate redeclaration of backend types.ts rather than a shared package or generated client. The two large test files (604 and 486 lines) are excluded by the rubric's note that test quality is out of scope.
